preamble: The diphthong [aʊ] reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed syllables, a phonetically natural process where the complex articulation of the diphthong simplifies to the neutral schwa vowel. This is a stress-conditioned reduction pattern documented across Germanic languages.

examples.note: Words like 'how' in weakly stressed positions may reduce the [aʊ] diphthong to schwa in rapid connected speech.
Reduction pattern observed in rapid or casual speech contexts where stress is minimal.
